meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master SaaS development as a student. Use this intermediate guide to bypass technical barriers using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the lack of SaaS knowledge requires moving beyond tutorials to a modular starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can bridge the gap between abstract theory and production-ready architecture, effectively launching a functional SaaS product in record time.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the ultimate adversary. For a student, the chasm between writing a simple script and deploying a multi-tenant SaaS application feels insurmountable, often ending in tutorial hell where knowledge remains theoretical and dormant. You possess the drive, but you lack the battle-tested framework to turn logic into a recurring revenue engine.

This guide is your tactical bridge. We are stripping away the fluff of traditional development to focus on a high-velocity SaaS starter kit. By integrating Antigravity’s architectural scaffolding with the cognitive power of Claude Code, you will stop simulating progress and start building real-world infrastructure that scales.

steps.title: Define Multi-tenant Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundational database schema and authentication layer required for a SaaS product to support multiple independent users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a multi-tenant PostgreSQL schema with Row Level Security (RLS) policies for a SaaS starter kit, including tables for organizations, users, and subscriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate RLS policies for a multi-tenant SaaS schema using Supabase or Prisma.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Integration
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe to handle subscription tiers and webhooks, the lifeblood of any SaaS project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe checkout session handler and webhook listener that updates the user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install stripe && npx stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks'

steps.title: Build the Authenticated Dashboard
steps.description: Develop a protected user interface where your customers can manage their settings and view their data securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js dashboard layout with protected routes, sidebar navigation, and a user profile management component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/app/dashboard/page.tsx --instruction 'Add server-side session validation to this dashboard route.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Configure CI/CD to ensure that every push to your repository triggers an automated test and production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deploys the application to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for a Next.js project on Vercel.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to rapidly iterate on your core value proposition based on user feedback loops.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an AI-powered feature module that takes user input and uses an LLM to generate a specific output relevant to our SaaS niche.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Add AI-powered feature module using OpenAI SDK'

pro_tips: Always prioritize Row Level Security (RLS) before writing your first API route to ensure data isolation.
Use mock billing data in development environments to avoid triggering real Stripe API limits during testing.
Keep your database migrations small and version-controlled to avoid production deployment failures.
Leverage Claude Code's context-awareness by pointing it to your documentation folder before running complex refactors.
Focus on 'feature flags' early so you can deploy incomplete features without breaking the production experience.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, focus on setting up an analytics dashboard to track user behavior and identify which features drive the most engagement.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build anything from a niche micro-SaaS to a complex enterprise platform. The barrier isn't the code; it's the lack of a repeatable system. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
